label: 290_leadership transitions_elections_democracy_democratic resilience
description: The state of democracy in Africa is examined, highlighting both progress and challenges. Despite advances, long-term leadership and electoral processes face new complexities as the continent navigates democratic resilience.

earliest_articles.title: @ Brookings Podcast: African Elections and Democracy
earliest_articles.excerpt: Nations across Africa are holding elections, a good sign for emerging democracies on the continent. However, expert Melvin Ayogu says the quality of governance in African nations varies widely. Some states are making solid progress toward becoming effective democracies, while others remain under the sway of corrupt…

newest_articles.title: Improving democratic resilience in Africa: Lessons from comparative case studies
newest_articles.excerpt: Below is Chapter 4 of the 2026 Foresight Africa report, which brings together leading scholars and practitioners to illuminate how Africa can navigate the challenges of 2026 and chart a path toward inclusive, resilient, and self-determined growth. central_articles.excerpt: Editor’s note: This piece was originally published in Spanish in a series of essays for the January/March 2016 issue of La Vanguardia. central_articles.title: From strong men to strong institutions: An assessment of Africa’s transition towards more political contestability
central_articles.excerpt: As President Obama said during his recent address at the African Union, “There’s a lot that I’d like to do to keep America moving.
